scenarios.opening_query: Dad's storage unit auctioned off before we even knew he had one, and family heirlooms are gone.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Probate || Consumer Law > Storage Units
scenarios.hidden_fact: The facility never followed the lien-notice statute — they mailed nothing to his address of record and auctioned within weeks; the unlawful-sale claim against the facility is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The facility skipped the required lien notices entirely and auctioned within weeks — the unlawful sale is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether lien-sale notice violations create the claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Their notices were proper — the failure was ours; recovering value now runs through the estate's administration and its claims.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Their notices were proper — the recovery question belongs to the estate's administration.

scenarios.opening_query: Mom made me joint owner of her house 'to keep it simple' and my siblings are furious now that she's gone.
scenarios.hidden_fact: They've filed a constructive-trust suit claiming the deed was only for convenience — defending my survivorship title in that case is where this lives now.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They filed a constructive-trust suit saying the deed was convenience only — defending my title in that case is where this lives.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a filed suit contests the survivorship deed
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nobody's filed anything — the rest of her estate still needs administering, and the house passes outside it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No one's filed — the rest of the estate just needs administering, and the house passes outside it.

scenarios.opening_query: My husband's pension check still arrives every month even though he passed in the spring. I'm afraid to touch it.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Probate || Labor & Employment > Pensions/Benefits/ERISA - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The plan says I should be getting a survivor annuity instead — the administrator botched the conversion, and fixing my survivor election with the plan is the case, not the estate.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The plan owes me a survivor annuity — they botched the conversion. Fixing my election with the administrator is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether plan-administration errors govern the benefit
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no survivor benefit — the overpaid checks are the estate's problem to return through administration.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: There's no survivor benefit — the overpayments are the estate's to return properly.

scenarios.opening_query: The house Mom left us has a tenant with a lease Mom signed that runs three more years at half market rent.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The estate's actually closed — my sister and I own it as landlords now, and managing or ending that below-market tenancy is landlord work.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The estate closed — my sister and I are the landlords now, and the below-market tenancy is ours to manage.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether title passed making it a landlord matter
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The estate's still open — the personal representative has to decide whether the lease binds the estate before anything else.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The estate's open — the representative has to sort whether the lease binds it first.

scenarios.opening_query: Grandma's caregiver claims Grandma 'gave' her the car and jewelry in her final weeks.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Elder Abuse || Wills & Trusts > Probate
scenarios.hidden_fact: There are signed gift letters, witnessed by her book club, from when she was demonstrably sharp — recovering anything means the estate contests them formally, and honestly they may stand.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: There are witnessed gift letters from when she was demonstrably sharp — the estate would have to contest them formally, and they may stand.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether documented capacity supports the gifts
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No documentation — and the 'gifts' happened the same week the doctor noted she couldn't recognize family; that's exploitation to unwind.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No documentation — and the 'gifts' came the week the doctor noted she couldn't recognize family.
scenarios.vetting_note: Documented-capacity fact can defeat the exploitation theory; honest both ways.

scenarios.opening_query: My brother, the executor, wants to sell Dad's gun collection but half of it may not be legal to sell.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Probate || Criminal Law > Gun Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: Two are NFA items with no paperwork and one heir is a felon who can't receive his bequest — firearms-law counsel has to clear transfers before the estate touches them.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Two are unpapered NFA items and one heir is a felon who can't take his bequest — firearms counsel has to clear transfers first.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether firearms-transfer law gates the distribution
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They're all ordinary hunting rifles with clean provenance — a standard estate appraisal and sale.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: All ordinary hunting rifles, clean provenance — standard estate sale.
